# Create CSV Test Dataset

Refactor the xUnit test project located at `./src/workspace/calculator-xunit-testing/calculator.tests/` (`calculator.tests.csproj`). The project currently uses `[InlineData]` attributes with hardcoded test values as its data source.

Migrate all data-driven test cases to a CSV file named `TestCases.csv` located in the test project root. The CSV must have the following columns: `Operand1`, `Operand2`, `Operation`, `ExpectedResult`, with a header row. All other non-data-driven test cases should remain unchanged.

This prompt covers the CSV extraction step only. For the default runtime test data workflow, continue with `2.03-switch-test-data-to-pg` to use Testcontainers PostgreSQL with per-run randomized credentials.

**Related Skill:** See [create-csv-test-dataset skill](../skills/create-csv-test-dataset/SKILL.md) for detailed step-by-step implementation guidance, including folder/file setup, configuration, parsing logic, test method refactoring, troubleshooting, and quality checks.

**Fallback Implementation:** If the SKILL.md file cannot be read, implement the refactoring using the following baseline steps:

1. Add a `TestCases.csv` file to the test project root with the schema above.
2. Mark it as an embedded resource or set "Copy to Output Directory" to "Always" in the .csproj.
3. Parse it using CsvHelper (NuGet) or manual string splitting in a test helper method; if using CsvHelper with a model class, use a parameterless class with settable properties and import `CsvHelper.Configuration` for `ClassMap<T>`.
4. Replace inline `[InlineData]` attributes with public static `[MemberData]` providers that read the CSV, or implement a custom `[CsvData]` attribute.
5. Run `dotnet test` to verify all tests pass with the new data source.

## Follow-up

After CSV migration is complete, run [2.03-switch-test-data-to-pg.prompt.md](2.03-switch-test-data-to-pg.prompt.md) to move data-driven execution to the secure Testcontainers-backed path.
